domains.name: Preparing and Using Data for Analysis

questions.stem: A company needs near-real-time ingestion of application events with durable decoupling between producers and consumers. Which Google Cloud service is the best fit?
questions.explanation: Pub/Sub is the managed messaging service for decoupled event ingestion on Google Cloud. The other options do not provide the same streaming messaging role.

questions.stem: A team needs a globally consistent relational database with horizontal scale and SQL support. Which product should they choose?
questions.explanation: Cloud Spanner provides globally distributed relational storage with strong consistency and SQL. Bigtable is NoSQL, and the others are not relational databases.

questions.stem: Which TWO factors are most important when choosing between BigQuery and Bigtable? (Select TWO)

options.text: Analytical SQL versus low-latency key-value access patterns
Expected query style and access pattern
The number of browser tabs the team uses
Whether the company has a logo in blue

questions.explanation: BigQuery is optimized for analytical SQL, while Bigtable is optimized for low-latency key-based access. Browser tabs and logo color are irrelevant.

questions.stem: A retailer wants one architecture for both streaming click events and historical reprocessing using the same business logic. Which service is MOST aligned?
questions.explanation: Apache Beam on Dataflow provides a unified programming model for batch and streaming, which is ideal when the same logic must support both modes.

questions.stem: Why are partitioning and clustering often used together in BigQuery?

options.text: They reduce the amount of data scanned for many analytical queries
They eliminate the need for schema design

questions.explanation: Partitioning and clustering help BigQuery scan less data and improve performance/cost efficiency for many query patterns. They do not replace IAM or schema design.

questions.stem: What is a common reason to store raw landing-zone files in Cloud Storage?

options.text: It provides durable, low-cost object storage for many file formats
It is the only place BigQuery can read from

questions.explanation: Cloud Storage is a common low-cost landing zone for raw files. BigQuery can read many sources, and Cloud Storage does not automatically normalize schemas or replace governance.

questions.stem: A data platform team wants a managed governance layer for discovering, governing, and monitoring data across zones. Which service is MOST relevant?
questions.explanation: Dataplex is the relevant managed governance and data management layer across data lakes and warehouses. The other services address different domains.

questions.stem: A workload must serve sub-10 ms lookups for user profiles keyed by ID at massive scale. Why is BigQuery a poor primary store for this use case?

options.text: BigQuery is optimized for analytics, not ultra-low-latency key-based serving workloads

questions.explanation: BigQuery is an analytical warehouse, not a low-latency serving database. It supports SQL and string data, and Google manages the infrastructure.

questions.stem: What is the main benefit of a Pub/Sub dead-letter topic?

options.text: Messages that repeatedly fail delivery can be isolated for investigation
It makes every subscriber exactly-once by itself
It encrypts all payloads with customer-managed keys automatically

questions.explanation: Dead-letter topics capture repeatedly undeliverable messages for inspection and remediation. They do not by themselves provide exactly-once semantics, replace ack handling, or automatically configure CMEK.

questions.stem: A streaming pipeline reads from Pub/Sub and writes to BigQuery. Which service is best suited to apply event-time windowing and streaming transforms?
questions.explanation: Dataflow is the managed Beam runtime for streaming transformations including event-time windows. The other services are not the standard streaming transform engine for this pattern.

questions.stem: Which TWO ingestion services are commonly used for moving data into BigQuery from managed sources? (Select TWO)

options.text: Datastream for change data capture scenarios

questions.explanation: BigQuery Data Transfer Service and Datastream are relevant managed ingestion tools. IAP and Artifact Registry solve different problems.

questions.stem: A Dataflow job shows growing watermark lag because many events arrive late and out of order. Which remediation is MOST targeted?

options.text: Review event-time windowing, allowed lateness, and trigger behavior
Increase the number of BigQuery datasets
Convert the job to Cloud Functions

questions.explanation: Watermark lag is closely tied to event-time behavior, lateness, and trigger configuration. Dataset count and Cloud Functions do not address the root cause.

questions.stem: Why might a team choose Dataproc over Dataflow for a batch transformation?

options.text: They need managed Spark/Hadoop compatibility for an existing workload
They want to avoid all clusters and still run Spark unchanged

questions.explanation: Dataproc is the managed Spark/Hadoop service and is often chosen for compatibility with existing ecosystem workloads. The other options mismatch the requirement.

questions.stem: Which service is most appropriate for physically transferring very large on-premises datasets when network transfer is impractical?
questions.explanation: Transfer Appliance is designed for offline bulk data transfer when network migration is impractical. The other services are unrelated.

questions.stem: A streaming ingestion design must absorb temporary consumer outages without losing messages. Which Pub/Sub property matters most?

options.text: Durable decoupling between publishers and subscribers
Automatic BigQuery partition creation in every case

questions.explanation: Pub/Sub decouples producers and consumers durably so subscribers can recover after outages. The other options are not core Pub/Sub properties.

questions.stem: A Beam pipeline performs a huge group-by and workers hit memory pressure. Which change is a strong first mitigation?

options.text: Use managed shuffle features and review key skew
Store outputs in local files only

questions.explanation: Large group-by stages often benefit from managed shuffle and skew review. Renaming topics or disabling autoscaling does not address shuffle pressure.

questions.stem: Which BigQuery feature is most directly used to organize data by date so queries can scan only relevant slices?
questions.explanation: Partitioned tables divide data into partitions, often by date/time, so queries can scan only relevant slices. The other features serve unrelated purposes.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of clustering in BigQuery?

options.text: To colocate related rows based on clustered columns for more efficient query pruning
To replicate data across clouds automatically

questions.explanation: Clustering organizes storage by clustered columns so BigQuery can scan less data for qualifying predicates. It does not create subscriptions, cross-cloud replication, or schemas.

questions.stem: Which TWO Bigtable schema design practices are usually recommended? (Select TWO)

options.text: Design row keys to distribute traffic and avoid hotspotting
Keep access patterns in mind when designing keys and families
Use random giant blobs in one column family for every workload
Assume SQL joins will be the main access path

questions.explanation: Bigtable schemas should reflect access patterns and avoid hotspotting through good key design. Giant blobs and SQL joins are poor default assumptions.

questions.stem: A team stores high-cardinality time-series telemetry and needs predictable millisecond reads by key. Which store is usually the better fit than BigQuery?
questions.explanation: Bigtable is designed for very large-scale low-latency key-based reads and writes. BigQuery is optimized for analytics, not primary millisecond serving.

questions.stem: Why are materialized views useful in BigQuery?

options.text: They can speed up repeated queries by precomputing and maintaining results for supported patterns
They are only for exporting CSV files

questions.explanation: Materialized views can improve performance for repeated query patterns by maintaining precomputed results. They do not replace all base tables or create messaging resources.

questions.stem: When would external tables over Cloud Storage be attractive?

options.text: When you want to query supported files in place before or instead of loading them fully
When you need sub-millisecond transactional updates
When you want to avoid object storage completely

questions.explanation: External tables are useful for querying supported files in place. They are not transactional serving stores and do not provide HBase APIs.

questions.stem: A team needs to retain raw immutable files and curated analytics tables. Which combination fits well?

options.text: Cloud Storage for raw files and BigQuery for curated analytics
Bigtable for dashboards and Gmail for raw files
Pub/Sub for long-term storage and Dataflow for SQL serving

questions.explanation: Cloud Storage is a common raw landing zone, and BigQuery is a common curated analytics store. The other combinations do not fit the requirement.

questions.stem: Why can a poorly chosen Bigtable row key cause severe performance problems?

options.text: Sequential or skewed keys can create hotspots on a narrow part of the keyspace
It turns Bigtable into a columnar warehouse

questions.explanation: Poor key distribution can hotspot tablets and hurt latency and throughput. The other statements are incorrect.

questions.stem: What does BigQuery ML allow analysts and engineers to do?

options.text: Create and use certain ML models directly with SQL in BigQuery
Replace Vertex AI in every scenario

questions.explanation: BigQuery ML enables model creation and inference for supported ML workflows directly in SQL. It does not replace every other ML service or unrelated data products.

questions.stem: A BI team repeatedly filters by customer segment and month. What data-modeling choice in BigQuery can help most?

options.text: Use partitioning and clustering aligned to common predicates
Store the table as one unsorted daily export forever

questions.explanation: Aligning partitioning and clustering to common predicates helps BigQuery prune scanned data. The other choices do not optimize query access.

questions.stem: Which TWO steps improve data readiness for downstream analytics? (Select TWO)

options.text: Define clear schemas and data quality expectations
Model tables to match important analytical access patterns
Rely on accidental field naming conventions only

questions.explanation: Explicit schemas/quality expectations and analytical modeling both improve data readiness. Accidental naming and undocumented transformations increase confusion.

questions.stem: A team wants a semantic layer of trusted definitions and governed assets across lake and warehouse data. Which service aligns best?
questions.explanation: Dataplex is the relevant governed data management layer. The other services address networking or infrastructure placement.

questions.stem: Why should transformation logic be documented and versioned?

options.text: It helps teams reproduce datasets and understand changes over time
It forces every query to be free

questions.explanation: Documented, versioned transformation logic supports reproducibility and team understanding. It does not remove optimization or security needs.

questions.stem: What is a good reason to create curated tables instead of pointing every dashboard at raw files?

options.text: Curated tables improve consistency, performance, and semantic clarity for analytics
Curated tables remove all storage cost

questions.explanation: Curated tables provide cleaner semantics and often better performance for analytics than raw landing files. The other claims are false.

questions.stem: A team is deciding between BigQuery ML and Vertex AI for an initial forecasting prototype already centered on SQL analysts. Which first choice is reasonable?

options.text: Start with BigQuery ML because the workflow stays close to SQL and warehouse data
Start by building a custom distributed training cluster manually

questions.explanation: For SQL-centric teams and supported model types, BigQuery ML is a sensible first choice. The other options add unnecessary complexity or omit evaluation.

questions.stem: A data mart contains columns that almost no analytical query uses, but scans remain expensive. Which action is MOST targeted?

options.text: Review table design and query patterns to reduce unnecessary scanned data, including partition and cluster choices

questions.explanation: Expensive scans should lead to storage/query design review, especially partitioning, clustering, and table shape. Cosmetic or governance changes do not solve scan cost.

questions.stem: Why is monitoring important for production data pipelines?

options.text: It helps detect failures, regressions, and cost issues before they become larger incidents
It removes the need for IAM

questions.explanation: Monitoring is essential for operational visibility. It complements rather than replaces design, testing, and IAM.

questions.stem: What is a key benefit of Dataproc autoscaling for cluster-based batch workloads?

options.text: It can adjust cluster size to workload demand and improve cost efficiency
It turns Dataproc into a serverless message queue
It replaces Spark jobs with SQL views

questions.explanation: Autoscaling helps match cluster resources to workload demand, which can improve efficiency. It does not replace Spark semantics or guarantee no failures.

questions.stem: Which TWO signals are valuable to monitor for a production analytics platform? (Select TWO)

options.text: Query cost and resource consumption trends

questions.explanation: Operational failures/lag and cost/resource trends are important production signals. Font preferences and color schemes are not.

questions.stem: A pipeline change improves speed but produces subtly wrong aggregations. What does this illustrate?

options.text: Operational performance improvements must still be validated for correctness
Faster pipelines are always more accurate
Data quality checks are optional in batch systems

questions.explanation: Performance gains do not guarantee correctness, so data quality validation remains necessary. The other statements are unsafe assumptions.

questions.stem: Why should teams automate repeatable pipeline deployment steps?

options.text: Automation reduces drift and makes releases more reproducible
Automation removes the need for review

questions.explanation: Automation reduces inconsistency and improves reproducibility. It does not eliminate reviews or guarantee no downtime.

questions.stem: A job succeeds technically but arrives after the business SLA. What category of issue is this?

options.text: A reliability/operational issue because timeliness is part of service quality
Not an issue because the query eventually finished

questions.explanation: Meeting SLAs is part of production reliability. Late data can be as harmful as failed data for business outcomes.

questions.stem: What is a strong reason to keep representative test datasets for pipeline changes?

options.text: They help catch regressions before production rollout
They remove the need for monitoring
They guarantee no schema changes ever occur

questions.explanation: Representative test datasets help surface regressions pre-release. They complement monitoring and source control rather than replace them.

questions.stem: A team has many independent data products and wants a consistent governance and operational view across them. Which service helps most?
questions.explanation: Dataplex provides a consistent governance-oriented view across distributed data assets. The other services do not serve that role.
